title: Maanga Maanga Song Lyrics Pratap
stanzas:
ye mangaa, maangaa
ye mangaa, maangaa
ye mangaa, maangaa

naakkazhagu beetroot-u
mookkazhagu carrot-u
neeye oru market-u
edhukku vandha menaketu

seththa summaa irungoda
sutti pasangalaa
nee vaa kuzhandha
nee vaamaa

Maangaa maangaa
rendu maangaa
markettu pogaatha
gundu maangaa gaa

poosanikkaa kidaikkala
vazhathandu kidaikkala
urula kizhangu kidaikkala
pacha muzhagaa kidaikkala

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Maanga Maanga?

The song Maanga Maanga conveys a sense of joy and celebration, reflecting the playful nature of life.

What is the plot context of the movie Pratap?

The movie Pratap revolves around themes of love and friendship, with the song highlighting moments of happiness.

Who is the singer of Maanga Maanga?

The song is sung by the legendary SPB, known for his soulful voice in Tamil cinema.

Who composed the music for Maanga Maanga?

The music for the song was composed by Maragatha Mani, adding a unique flavor to the track.

When was Maanga Maanga released?

The song was released on April 16, 1993, and has since become a beloved classic.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In the film Pratap, the song Maanga Maanga serves as a vibrant celebration of life and joy. It encapsulates the playful spirit of the characters, inviting listeners to reminisce about happy moments. The lively lyrics by Vairamuthu, combined with SPB's enchanting voice, evoke a sense of nostalgia and warmth. This melody is perfect for celebrations, making it a timeless favorite among fans of Tamil music.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Maanga Maanga is a delightful melody that captures the essence of joy and nostalgia, showcasing the legendary voice of SPB. The collaboration with lyricist Vairamuthu and music director Maragatha Mani brings a unique charm to this classic from the film Pratap. Dive into the vibrant world of this song that resonates with the heart and soul of Tamil music lovers.
stanzaMeanings: Two mangoes, two mangoes, the round mangoes that don't go to the market.
Hey, beautiful mango, don't tease my eyes.
Like seeing the town, it feels like a town inside you.
Two mangoes, two mangoes, the round mangoes that don't go to the market.
Oh mango, mango, oh mango, mango, oh mango, mango.
I don't want bitter gourd, just your fingers are enough.
I want Kovai fruit, just a little of that is enough.
The color of the tomato is like your eyes, little one.
The ripe ones are seen first, then the unripe ones are picked.
The sweet beetroot and the fragrant carrot, why did you come to this market?
Two mangoes, two mangoes, the round mangoes that don't go to the market.
Just stay calm, you little kids, come here, are you a child?
Two mangoes, two mangoes, the round mangoes that don't go to the market.
Two mangoes, two mangoes.
Oh God, what should I do?
Like the sweet mango, the unripe ones are not available, not available.
Like your long fingers, the ripe ones are not available, not available.
The potatoes that are like you are not available.
The green chilies that are like your eyes are not available.
The ripe ones are not available, the long ones are not available, the potatoes are not available, the green chilies are not available.
Searching for vegetables, what is my eye suffering for?
What you don't have is in the market.
Two mangoes, two mangoes, the round mangoes that don't go to the market.
Oh mango, oh mango, oh mango.
